functions: Tumor suppressor
Regulation of apoptosis
Cell cycle checkpoint control
Transcription factor activity
DNA damage response
Maintenance of genomic stability
pathways: TP53 Regulates Transcription of Cell Cycle Genes
pubmed.druggability_notes: The MDM2-p53 antagonist brigimadlin was tested versus doxorubicin in MDM2-amplified dedifferentiated liposarcoma (Brightline-1, NCT05218499); brigimadlin activated the TP53 pathway with a 22.3% objective response rate, though the PFS primary endpoint was not met. p53 immunohistochemistry staining patterns (null/loss, strong diffuse nuclear positivity, wild-type scattered weak) serve as a surrogate for TP53 gene mutation status to distinguish clonally independent tumors. Germline pathogenic TP53 variants are an NCCN-recognized prostate cancer risk gene.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that activates or represses target genes upon binding to its response element
Induces cell cycle arrest via transactivation of cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor (e.g. CDKN1A/p21)
Induces apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression and repression of BCL2
Promotes DNA repair in response to DNA damage
Activates oxidative stress-induced necrosis in cooperation with mitochondrial PPIF, largely transcription-independent
Induces transcription of lincRNA-p21 mediating TP53-dependent transcriptional repression and apoptosis
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity within the CAK complex to halt cell cycle progression after DNA damage
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transactivation of PER2
pathways: TP53 Regulates Transcription of Cell Cycle Genes
TP53 Regulates Transcription of Genes Involved in Cytochrome C Release
uniprot.druggability_notes: Contains a zinc ion-binding/metal-binding DNA-binding domain; 3D structures available. Activity is modulated by interaction with MDM2/MDM4 family proteins and by ASPP-family regulators (PPP1R13B/ASPP1, TP53BP2/ASPP2 activate while PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibits pro-apoptotic activity), representing potential interfaces for therapeutic modulation.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that activates target genes inducing cell cycle arrest, DNA repair or apoptosis
Tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest or apoptosis depending on physiological context and cell type
Negatively regulates cell division by controlling expression of genes required for cell division
Induces apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression or repression of Bcl-2 expression
Activates oxidative stress-induced necrosis in cooperation with mitochondrial PPIF, largely transcription-independent
Induces transcription of long intergenic non-coding RNAs lincRNA-p21 and lincRNA-Mkln1
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity in CAK complex in response to DNA damage, halting cell cycle
Regulates circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transcription of PER2

disease_associations.disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

cellular_states: Senescent cells (oxidative stress and oncogene-induced senescence)
Cells in G1 and G2/M cell cycle arrest
opentargets.druggability_notes: Text describes loss of function in cancer due to loss of tetramerization ability (R-HSA-9723905), indicating relevance of the tetramerization domain. Pro-apoptotic activity is modulated by competitive interaction with ASPP family proteins (PPP1R13B/ASPP1, TP53BP2/ASPP2 activate; PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibits), suggesting a protein-protein interaction interface relevant to therapeutic modulation. No explicit druggability or small-molecule binding pocket statements provided.
